entity_name: Cooperative of American Physicians Independent Expenditure Committee

reasoning: United Auto Workers is a labor union (501(c)(5)), explicitly classified as labor-aligned per override rules, with FEC/OpenSecrets data showing heavy Democratic donations and endorsements of Biden/Harris, recent militant strikes, and progressive stances on labor and climate transition [OpenSecrets UAW Profile](https://www.opensecrets.org/orgs/united-auto-workers/totals?cycle=A&id=d000000070), [ProPublica UAW 990](https://projects.propublica.org/nonprofits/organizations/380679801).

No specific "Tim Geithner Political Operation" organization, PAC, or 501(c)(4) identified in FEC, IRS 990s, OpenSecrets, ProPublica, or news sources; Geithner is a finance executive (Warburg Pincus Chairman) with ~$10M net worth and no direct political donor entity linked [OpenSecrets](https://www.opensecrets.org/revolving-door/geithner-timothy/summary?id=78265), [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Timothy_Geithner).

entity_name: Renaissance Technologies and the 7 Billion Dollar Tax Settlement
reasoning: Renaissance Technologies is a quantitative hedge fund founded by billionaire Jim Simons ($30B+ net worth) managing $100B+ AUM, involved in a $7B IRS tax settlement over trading strategies, with employees/executives donating heavily to both Democrats (86% in 2024 per [OpenSecrets](https://www.opensecrets.org/orgs/renaissance-technologies/totals?id=D000022219)) and Republicans via figures like Robert Mercer, reflecting bipartisan influence-seeking and tax deregulation lobbying ([OpenSecrets](https://www.opensecrets.org/news/2016/06/a-hedge-fund-house-divided-renaissance-technologies/), [InfluenceWatch](https://www.influencewatch.org/for-profit/renaissance-technologies/)); NLRB charges indicate anti-union stance ([NLRB](https://www.nlrb.gov/case/04-CA-198349)).

Charles Koch, with over $70B net worth as co-owner of Koch Industries (fossil fuels, chemicals, refining), funds Republican causes via $40M+ to AFP Action, supports climate denial and anti-union efforts per OpenSecrets, Wikipedia, Greenpeace, Time, and In These Times.

Palantir Technologies, a data analytics firm with major government contracts in defense, intelligence, ICE, and military AI, fits military-industrial capital; its PAC gives ~70% to Republicans but also to Democrats including defense committee members like Rep. Adam Smith and Sen. The Gates Foundation is a private 501(c)(3) controlled by billionaire Bill Gates (>$100B net worth, tech-monopoly origins via Microsoft), channeling massive grants including to dark money vehicles like Arabella Advisors while funding education reforms criticized as anti-union and privatizing [OpenSecrets](https://www.opensecrets.org/orgs/bill-melinda-gates-foundation/summary?id=D000031958), [ProPublica](https://projects.propublica.org/nonprofits/organizations/562618866), [InfluenceWatch](https://www.influencewatch.org/non-profit/gates-foundation/), employee donations lean heavily Democratic.

Walton family controls ~45% of Walmart, world's largest retailer with family net worth exceeding $400B, fitting retail-monopoly capital owned by ruling-class billionaires; notorious anti-union history including store closures and NLRB violations; Walmart PAC donations split ~57% Rep/43% Dem; primarily supports union-busting and deregulation via lobbying and donations [[OpenSecrets PAC](https://www.opensecrets.org/political-action-committees-pacs/walmart-inc/C00093054/summary/2024), [OpenSecrets Donor Lookup](https://www.opensecrets.org/donor-lookup/results?name=walton), [NLRB/Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/walmart-engaged-illegal-union-busting-california-store-us-agency-says-2024-01-25/)]

Laurene Powell Jobs, widow of Steve Jobs with $12B+ net worth from Apple/Disney stakes, controls Emerson Collective (tech/media investments, progressive philanthropy in education/immigration/climate) and funds Democrats heavily (e.g., $771K DNC, $73K Harris [OpenSecrets](https://www.opensecrets.org/orgs/emerson-collective/recipients?id=D000068512)), fitting tech-monopoly capital, ruling-class position, corporate-liberal ideology, neutral on unions (no evidence of anti-union activity), and center-left alignment [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/billionaires/profiles/laurene-p-jobs/), [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laurene_Powell_Jobs), [CelebrityNetWorth](https://www.celebritynetworth.com/richest-businessmen/business-executives/laurene-powell-jobs-net-worth/).

The California Restaurant Association is a 501(c)(6) trade association representing 22,000+ mostly independent restaurants, operating PACs to support pro-industry candidates and fight regulations like PAGA abuse, AB 257 (union-linked), and employer speech limits on unions, aligning with business advocacy against labor mandates in Democrat-led California [[CRA site](https://www.calrest.org), [ProPublica](https://projects.propublica.org/nonprofits/organizations/951241045), [Newsroom](https://www.calrest.org/newsroom), [PACs](https://www.calrest.org/committees)].

Emily's List is a PAC and affiliated 501(c)(4) that bundles small-to-medium donations from professionals and some wealthy individuals (e.g., up to $100k gifts, no dominant billionaire control) to elect pro-choice Democratic women, cooperating with labor like AFL-CIO on candidates while focusing on reproductive rights and progressive causes without anti-union activity [OpenSecrets](https://www.opensecrets.org/orgs/emily-s-list/summary?id=d000000113), [FEC](https://www.fec.gov/data/committee/C00193433/), [ProPublica](https://projects.propublica.org/nonprofits/organizations/461067490), [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EMILYs_List).

Reid Hoffman, LinkedIn co-founder with $2.6B net worth, owns significant capital in tech giants like LinkedIn (sold to Microsoft), representing tech-monopoly capital; he is a major Democratic donor funding anti-Trump and mainstream Democrat PACs via [OpenSecrets](https://www.opensecrets.org/outside-spending/donor_detail/2024?id=U0000004136), exhibits corporate-liberal views with calls for deregulation (e.g., firing FTC's Lina Khan), pro-Zionist stance noted in coverage, and anti-union leanings inferred from tech exec criticism of unions and gig economy/AI predictions disrupting traditional labor per [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reid_Hoffman) and search results.

The Adelson family, led by Miriam Adelson with $35B+ net worth from Las Vegas Sands casino monopoly, are ruling-class retail-monopoly owners who donate hundreds of millions to hard-right Republican super PACs like Preserve America and dark money groups, actively oppose unions, and strongly support Zionist causes [[OpenSecrets](https://www.opensecrets.org/outside-spending/donor_detail/2024?id=U0000000310), [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Miriam_Adelson), [Peoples World](https://www.peoplesworld.org/article/billionaire-casino-owner-adelson-takes-aim-at-unions/)]

Marco Rubio, raising ~$900K in 2023-2024 primarily from upper-bourgeois donors in finance (e.g. [Steve Schwarzman, Blackstone](https://www.opensecrets.org/political-action-committees-pacs/reclaim-america-pac/C00500025/donors/2024)), lobbying, and business, donating 100% to Republicans including Trump 47 Cmte; supports standard GOP pro-capital policies but not a dark-money 501(c)4 vehicle or billionaire-dominated ([OpenSecrets](https://www.opensecrets.org/political-action-committees-pacs/reclaim-america-pac/C00500025/summary/2024), [FEC](https://www.fec.gov/data/committee/C00500025/)).

Ross Stevens is founder/CEO of Stone Ridge Asset Management (finance-capital, $20B+ AUM), with billionaire-level capacity shown by multiple $100M donations despite not on Forbes lists [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-03-05/ross-stevens-pledges-100-million-to-us-olympians-paralympians), [Fortune](https://fortune.com/2023/12/07/penn-100-million-gift-antisemitism-congressional-hearing/); withdrew UPenn donation over antisemitism (zionist-aligned), HRF bitcoin for dissidents (deregulation), firm lobbies securities, employees donated bipartisan with Dem lean but Bitcoin PAC [OpenSecrets](https://www.opensecrets.org/orgs/stone-ridge-asset-management/summary?id=D000103115); no union info (neutral), no personal donations found.

The NRA is a 501(c)(4) membership-based gun rights advocacy group and de facto trade association for the firearms industry, funded ~40% by contributions (some corporate from gun makers like Beretta, MidwayUSA [OpenSecrets](https://www.opensecrets.org/orgs/national-rifle-assn/summary?id=D000000082), [A-Mark Foundation](https://amarkfoundation.org/reports/who-funds-the-nra/)), ~40% program services/dues, spending heavily on Republican candidates, lobbying for firearm deregulation [ProPublica](https://projects.propublica.org/nonprofits/organizations/530116130); per override rules, trade associations are upper-bourgeois.

The California Charter Schools Association (CCSA), a 501(c)(3) with a linked 501(c)(4) Advocates arm sponsoring a PAC for political spending, promotes charter school expansion (privatization/deregulation), receives heavy contributions likely from upper-bourgeois tech donors like Reed Hastings (>$100M giver per OpenSecrets [OpenSecrets](https://www.opensecrets.org/donor-lookup/results?name=Reed+Hastings)), shows minimal direct FEC activity but state-level bipartisan donations, and opposes teachers unions as evident from CTA critiques and charter context [ProPublica CCSA](https://projects.propublica.org/nonprofits/organizations/510465703), [ProPublica Advocates](https://projects.propublica.org/nonprofits/organizations/262318098), [CCSA PAC](https://www.transparencyusa.org/ca/committee/charter-public-schools-pac-sponsored-by-california-charter-schools-association-advocates-1302433-rcp/payees).

The Riverside Sheriffs' Association is a 501(c)(5) labor organization and exclusive bargaining representative (union) for over 3,500 Riverside County sheriff's deputies and related employees, qualifying for labor override rules per [ProPublica Nonprofit Explorer](https://projects.propublica.org/nonprofits/organizations/956203844), [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/riverside-sheriffs-association), and [official site](https://www.rcdsa.org/about/political-action); it operates a PAC (Public Education Fund) supporting pro-law enforcement candidates bipartisanly but often Republicans like Sheriff Chad Bianco, aligning with carceral-state ideology typical of police unions per [academic research](https://academic.oup.com/bjc/article/62/1/90/6282889).

Trump Victory is a joint fundraising PAC ([FEC](https://www.fec.gov/data/committee/C00618389/)) for Trump's campaign, RNC, and state parties, funded by upper-bourgeois donors including billionaires like Woody Johnson ($6B+ net worth, [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Woody_Johnson)) and Diane Hendricks ($20B+, [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diane_Hendricks)), supporting Republican hard-right politics with deregulation and imperialism foci per donor profiles and Trump alignment ([OpenSecrets](https://www.opensecrets.org/joint-fundraising-committees-jfcs/trump-victory/C00618389/2020/donors)).

Governor, Senate nominee) primarily funded by labor unions (AFSCME, SEIU, IBEW, teachers, laborers) rather than corporate PACs (which she rejects), with progressive stances on justice reform, gun violence, reproductive rights, minimum wage, Medicare for All, and equity initiatives [[Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Juliana_Stratton), [Ballotpedia](https://ballotpedia.org/Juliana_Stratton), [WBEZ Campaign Cash](https://interactive.wbez.org/campaigncash/candidate/juliana-stratton/), [FEC](https://www.fec.gov/data/candidate/S6IL00458/), [OpenSecrets](https://www.opensecrets.org/officeholders/juliana-w-stratton/summary?cycle=2022&id=12431605)].
